response_text:
SCORES:

Architectural Soundness: 90/100

Business Viability: 91/100

Competitive Positioning: 93/100

Implementation Feasibility: 87/100

User Experience: 88/100

Security: 89/100

Scalability: 86/100

OVERALL: 89/100

FINDINGS (remaining issues only):

[Security] [P0] Uplift apps introduce a new untrusted-data-to-command boundary without a hard capability envelope. inbox/skills/, inbox/templates/, and recipe aliases like compose_uplift, inject_text, ensure_model, and save_to_outbox create a second execution plane where user/authored files can influence browser actions and third-party LLM prompts. That is a classic UNTRUSTED_DATA_EXECUTING_COMMANDS risk. EXACT FIX: add a signed manifest allowlist for every uplift skill/template. Only load files whose SHA-256 is present in approved_manifest.json generated at build time. Reject any runtime-added or modified skill/template with a hard error: UPLIFT_MANIFEST_MISMATCH. Do not auto-reload directories. Require explicit app restart after manifest update.

[Security] [P0] The uplift evidence chain is not fully sealed against prompt-content exfiltration. You hash-chain evidence files, but the spec does not define redaction rules for prompts, model outputs, or accidental pasted secrets. That violates SECRETS NEVER EXPOSED. EXACT FIX: before writing any file to outbox/responses/ or outbox/evidence/, run deterministic redaction on API keys, bearer tokens, cookies, email addresses, and 32+ char high-entropy strings. Persist both redacted_sha256 and original_sha256 in SQLite, but write only redacted payloads to disk unless the run is explicitly marked sensitive_debug=true by a Pro+ policy gate.

[Architectural Soundness] [P1] The VACUUM fix still couples DB maintenance to extension/service-worker state. This was the core flaw in Round 5, and the new handshake-based retry does not remove it; it just makes the coupling more elaborate. A localhost DB owned by the companion/server should not depend on Chromium alarms, SW resurrection, or NM reachability for maintenance. EXACT FIX: delete the Chromium alarm path entirely. Move maintenance into the Python server: background task every 24h checks db_size_mb, freelist_count, and idle status; runs PRAGMA optimize first, then VACUUM only if db_size_mb > 50 and freelist_ratio > 0.2 and no active tasks exist. Surface maintenance state to tray via localhost status API, not browser alarms.

[Implementation Feasibility] [P1] Key-handoff ack over stdout is under-specified and vulnerable to protocol ambiguity. If stdout is shared with logs or other process messages, the ack can race or be spoofed by malformed output. EXACT FIX: reserve stdout for NDJSON control frames only during bootstrap. First line MUST equal exactly:
{"type":"bootstrap_ack","status":"keys_loaded","providers":["openai","anthropic"]}
Any non-JSON or extra pre-ack output is fatal. After ack, switch logs to stderr only. Add failing test: “stdout log before bootstrap ack causes process abort.”

[Implementation Feasibility] [P1] PAUSED → ABANDONED on server restart is deterministic, but it is too coarse and can destroy recoverable work. This is fail-loud, which is good, but currently throws away the distinction between restart-safe and restart-unsafe tasks. EXACT FIX: add restart_policy to each task: resume_after_restart, replay_from_checkpoint, abandon_on_restart. Default replay_from_checkpoint. On restart, apply policy instead of blanket ABANDONED. Add migration default for existing rows.

[User Experience] [P1] “Re-enter API keys” on any handoff timeout is too generic and may misdiagnose a process/bootstrap bug as a user error. That violates FALLBACK-BAN by masking the actual failure class. EXACT FIX: surface exact state-specific errors:

BOOTSTRAP_ACK_TIMEOUT

BOOTSTRAP_ACK_INVALID_JSON

BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDER_MISMATCH

PYTHON_START_FAILED
Show “Re-enter API keys” only for provider mismatch or missing keys. Show “Restart Solace” for ack timeout/start failure.

[Scalability] [P1] The uplift app inbox/outbox design lacks concurrency control and retention policy. Multiple runs writing to shared directories can cause interleaving, hash-chain ambiguity, and disk growth. EXACT FIX: namespace every uplift run under immutable run folders:
outbox/{run_id}/responses/, outbox/{run_id}/evidence/
with a per-run append-only chain.ndjson. Add file lock on run creation. Retention policy: keep 30 days by default, configurable by tier, with storage quota enforcement and explicit EVIDENCE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED hard failure.

[Security] [P1] inject_text is a high-risk action alias because it can target arbitrary prompt boxes in external LLM UIs. Without a stricter target policy, this is silent capability expansion via recipe content. EXACT FIX: restrict inject_text to a fixed selector allowlist per uplift app and per supported model UI version. Require target verification via DOM fingerprint before injection. If fingerprint mismatch, fail with UNSUPPORTED_MODEL_UI_VERSION; do not attempt fuzzy selector fallback.

[Architectural Soundness] [P1] The new recipe aliases are a breaking expansion of the sealed recipe grammar, but no API/version lock is defined. PRIME-CODER requires API surface lock. EXACT FIX: bump recipe schema to recipe_version: 2. Maintain v1 parser in read-only compatibility mode. Any recipe using compose_uplift, hash_evidence, or wait_for_stable must declare v2 or be rejected with UNSUPPORTED_RECIPE_VERSION.

[Security] [P1] wait_for_stable is underspecified and can become an unbounded hidden IO/wait state. That violates HIDDEN_IO and missing-error-path rules. EXACT FIX: define wait_for_stable schema with required:

selectors: [1..5]

stability_window_ms max 5000

timeout_ms max 15000

poll_interval_ms fixed 250
On timeout, raise WAIT_FOR_STABLE_TIMEOUT and checkpoint failure reason. No infinite waits, no silent retries.

[Implementation Feasibility] [P2] The claim “all recipe JSON files validate” is UNVERIFIED without executable proof. Under PRIME-CODER, that cannot be treated as done. EXACT FIX: add CI job recipe-schema-test that loads every recipe file and validates against the exact JSON Schema for its declared version. Publish machine-readable artifact listing pass/fail per file. Block merge on any failure.

[Business Viability] [P2] The uplift apps as Pro-only value is strategically good, but the packaging is still muddy. “Optimization service” is not yet crisp enough for buyers versus “managed LLM” in Starter. EXACT FIX: define SKU boundaries in one table used across product, billing, and docs:

Free: local automation only

Starter: managed LLM only

Pro: uplift + evidence + deterministic replay export

Team: multi-seat + shared recipes/evidence

Enterprise: SSO + retention controls + policy packs
Reject any manifest whose required_tier exceeds active subscription with TIER_CAPABILITY_DENIED.

[User Experience] [P2] The maintenance overdue tray warning lacks an operator action path. Alert without resolution path increases support load. EXACT FIX: tray item must include:

“Run maintenance now”

“View DB status”

“Export diagnostics”
If maintenance cannot run, surface exact code NM_UNAVAILABLE, ACTIVE_TASKS_PRESENT, or DISK_SPACE_LOW.

[Scalability] [P2] Evidence-heavy uplift runs can dominate disk and hash time, but no backpressure policy is defined. EXACT FIX: add per-run evidence budget:

max 200 files

max 100MB

max 10 screenshots unless debug_mode=true
Hard-fail with EVIDENCE_BUDGET_EXCEEDED. Do not silently drop evidence.

[Security] [P2] The claim “>30% user drop-off at OS security warnings” is UNVERIFIED. It may be directionally plausible, but it is not proven in the supplied spec. EXACT FIX: keep notarization/signing as mandatory, but remove the numeric drop-off claim from ADRs/roadmaps unless backed by internal funnel data or cited research.

[Competitive Positioning] [P2] AIDB cross-session workflow memory is potentially differentiating, but the security and consent boundary is UNVERIFIED and underspecified. Cross-session memory can become hidden state that changes behavior without visible user approval. EXACT FIX: require memory writes to produce a visible “Save to workflow memory?” approval event on first capture per workflow class, with inspectable diff and delete controls. Memory reads must be surfaced in the evidence chain as memory_context_applied.

IMPROVEMENTS SINCE ROUND 5:

The five Round 5 fixes mostly address the earlier issues, but not all of them fully. The best fix is the stdin handoff acknowledgment; the weakest is still DB maintenance through Chromium alarm orchestration.

The PAUSED timeout addition is operationally cleaner. It closes the missing stale-session path and makes post-disconnect state explicit.

The macOS notarization and Windows signing additions are strong and practical. They improve installation trust and are the right pre-launch requirement.

The AI strategy ADR is useful. BYOK model selection and DOM compression strengthen differentiation, though cross-session memory needs stricter consent controls.

The uplift app concept is commercially smart. It creates a real premium feature surface instead of a vague “better prompts” story.

The recipe parser expansion is directionally fine, but it now needs schema versioning, tighter capability limits, and executable proof in CI.

VERDICT: NEEDS WORK

This is not ready for final sign-off under the loaded rules.

Why it dropped from PASS:

the uplift system introduced a new P0 trust boundary that is not sealed,

the DB maintenance flaw was not actually fixed at the ownership boundary,

and several new behaviors violate the loaded rules unless versioned, bounded, and made fail-loud.

The highest-priority fixes before engineering should be implemented in this order:

Seal uplift manifests and redact uplift evidence

Move DB maintenance into the server/companion, not Chromium alarms

Version-lock the recipe grammar and hard-bound inject_text / wait_for_stable

Make bootstrap stdout a strict protocol, not mixed output

Add explicit restart policies for paused tasks

Once those five are done, this likely moves back into the 91–93 overall range.
